what_it_does: Records a metadata attachment entry that links the design-assets channel to a target image asset.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
standouts: Target URI uses an image:// scheme with a space and parentheses in the asset name
File is a pure metadata stub with no embedded payload
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Keeps attachment metadata separate from the actual asset
concerns: Target string contains a literal space and parentheses, which may complicate parsing or URL encoding
No checksum, MIME type, size, or resolution for the referenced image
No title, caption, or alt text for accessibility/context
No indication whether the referenced image actually exists
notes: A lightweight ledger stub that registers an image attachment for the design-assets channel.
questions: Is the image:// URI resolvable by the consuming system?
Why is there no title, alt text, or asset checksum?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches what it does: it is a typed attachment record pointing to an image.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a durable, timestamped association between a channel and an external image asset without embedding the image itself.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed reference from a channel to an image asset, supporting attachment tracking independent of where the binary asset is stored.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment record named att-a350729f in the design-assets channel that points to image://design-assets/icons/image (28) and records an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register an image attachment in the design-assets channel for later retrieval or display.
